runs.name: Next: founder vague positioning
runs.responsePreview:
Salesforce “Agentforce” is Salesforce, Inc.’s push to make **agentic AI a first-class, packaged capability inside the Salesforce platform** (alongside CRM data, workflows, security, and distribution). {{fact:ground_truth:SALESFORCE}} In positioning terms, it raises the bar: buyers will increasingly expect “agents” to be native in their system-of-record, not bolted on.

What that means for *your* positioning (assuming you’re selling an AI/agent product in or around CRM/RevOps/service workflows):

### 1) Don’t position as “we add AI agents to Salesforce”
That becomes a commodity claim as Salesforce makes “agents” part of the suite narrative. Instead, position on **one of these defensible axes**:
- **System-of-record adjacency you own** (data you uniquely control or unify) {{fact:market_landscape:own_data_advantage}}
- **Outcome ownership** (measurable business KPI you take responsibility f

Turn the 3 counter-positioning moves into a one-page battlecard: ICP, wedge workflow, proof points required, and disqualifying questions for Salesforce-led deals.

- GPT‑4o: **$2.50 / 1M input**, **$10.00 / 1M output** (linkupSearch references OpenAI docs). (Source: linkupSearch [OpenAI pricing / docs])

**Anthropic (official):**
- Claude Sonnet 4.5: **$3 / 1M input**, **$15 / 1M output**. (Source: linkupSearch → “Pricing - Claude

runs.name: Next: founder vague positioning
runs.responsePreview:
Salesforce “Agentforce” is Salesforce, Inc.’s push to make **agentic AI a first-class, packaged capability inside the Salesforce platform** (alongside CRM data, workflows, security, and distribution). {{fact:ground_truth:SALESFORCE}} In positioning terms, it raises the bar: buyers will increasingly expect “agents” to be native in their system-of-record, not bolted on.

What that means for *your* positioning (assuming you’re selling an AI/agent product in or around CRM/RevOps/service workflows):

### 1) Don’t position as “we add AI agents to Salesforce”
That becomes a commodity claim as Salesforce makes “agents” part of the suite narrative. Instead, position on **one of these defensible axes**:
- **System-of-record adjacency you own** (data you uniquely control or unify) {{fact:market_landscape:own_data_advantage}}
- **Outcome ownership** (measurable business KPI you take responsibility f

Expand credible coverage set beyond VentureBeat (e.g., FT/WSJ/The Information/major analyst notes) using additional linkupSearch passes focused on 'Agentforce' and 'earnings transcript'.
Turn the 3 counter-positioning moves into a one-page battlecard: ICP, wedge workflow, proof points required, and disqualifying questions for Salesforce-led deals.

The wedge is “autonomous digital labor” (multi-step work execution) rather than a generic “copilot/chat UI.” {{fact:executive_summary:agentforce_positioning

runs.name: Pack: exec cross-provider pricing comparison
runs.responsePreview:
{{fact:ground_truth:GEMINI_3}}

Below is a practical “agent loop” comparison across **OpenAI vs Anthropic vs Google Gemini 3**, with **3 usage scenarios**, **estimated monthly cost**, and a recommendation. I’m optimizing for an **ENTERPRISE_EXEC** persona (standardization + cost + procurement risk), assuming you’re building production agent workflows.

## 0) Official pricing inputs (from web search) + ground-truth constraint
**OpenAI (official):**
- GPT‑4.1: **$2.00 / 1M input**, **$0.50 / 1M cached input**, **$8.00 / 1M output**. (Source: OpenAI pricing + model docs via linkupSearch [OpenAI Pricing; GPT‑4.1 Model doc])  
- GPT‑4o: **$2.50 / 1M input**, **$10.00 / 1M output** (linkupSearch references OpenAI docs). (Source: linkupSearch [OpenAI pricing / docs])

**Anthropic (official):**
- Claude Sonnet 4.5: **$3 / 1M input**, **$15 / 1M output**. (Source: linkupSearch → “Pricing - Claude

risks: Target biology risk: surface expression may not translate to therapeutic index due to heterogeneity/off-tumor expression.
ADC execution risk: linker/payload/DAR and stability can drive tox or limit efficacy.
Translatability risk: mapping outputs may not mirror in-human tumor microenvironment behavior.
Competitive crowding in ADC/bispecific space can compress differentiation and partnering economics.
Financing/burn risk if multiple programs advance before value-inflection data.
